description: Embark on a breathtaking journey through the scenic wonders of North India as you explore the hill stations and heritage gems of Shimla, Manali, Dharamshala, Dalhousi, and Amritsar. Begin amidst the colonial charm of Shimla, stroll along the Mall Road, and soak in panoramic Himalayan views. Continue to Manali, where snow-capped peaks, Solang Valley adventures, and the serenity of Hadimba Temple await. In Dharamshala, experience the peaceful Tibetan culture and the spiritual aura of the Dalai Lama’s temple. Discover the pine-clad beauty and British-era architecture of Dalhousi, followed by a soulful conclusion in Amritsar, home to the shimmering Golden Temple and the patriotic spectacle at the Wagah Border. This trip beautifully blends nature, adventure, spirituality, and culture — creating memories that last a lifetime.

Anything not mentioned in inclusions

faqs.title: 1. What is the best time to visit Shimla–Manali–Dharamshala–Dalhousie–Amritsar?
faqs.content: The best time is March–June for pleasant weather and sightseeing. For snow, visit in December–February. Monsoon (July–September) offers greenery but occasional roadblocks.

faqs.title: 2. What are the must-visit attractions in this circuit?
faqs.content:
Shimla: Kufri, Mall Road, Jakhoo Temple

Manali: Solang Valley, Atal Tunnel, Rohtang Pass (permit needed)

Dharamshala: McLeodganj, Dalai Lama Temple

Dalhousie: Khajjiar (Mini Switzerland)

Amritsar: Golden Temple, Wagah Border, Jallianwala Bagh

faqs.title: 3. Is this trip suitable for families and senior citizens?
faqs.content: Yes, it is a family-friendly circuit. Some high-altitude areas like Rohtang/Solang require caution for seniors due to cold and walking distances.

faqs.title: 4. What type of clothing should I carry?
faqs.content: Carry warm clothes, jackets, thermals, gloves, and good shoes for Shimla–Manali. For Amritsar, normal clothing is fine. In winter, heavy woollens are mandatory.

faqs.title: 5. Are permits required for Rohtang Pass or Atal Tunnel?
faqs.content:
Rohtang Pass requires a special permit, available online (seasonal).
Atal Tunnel generally does not require a permit but depends on weather conditions.
